title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Cutt arrives to bring medicine to Sir Osbert and confesses she only realized he had been out for a walk after Mr. Betman inquired about his whereabouts. Sir Osbert admits he went out for fresh air due to insomnia and re-entered the house through a window while wearing only slippers. Following Mrs. Cutt's departure, George Lomax suggests the shooter wounded Jimmy Cecil and escaped via the gravel path along the terrace. However, Inspector Butler disputes this, stating he arrived at the terrace less than fifty seconds after the shot and saw no one. Butler theorizes the culprit escaped by climbing the ivy and suggests the man used a path through Mr. Orrick’s locked room and through Stanley’s room to reach an exit.

Inspector Bates suggests the culprit might be an insider, a possibility that distresses George Lomax. Jimmy Cecil then asks about a charred, oversized object on the table, which Inspector Batter identifies as a burnt glove found in a fireplace grate that appears to have been chewed by a dog.

Eileen Bündel enters and reports that her father's servant, John Boll, has gone missing. Sir Osbert identifies Boll as the likely culprit, noting that Boll is a well-trained man who had been at the Chimney House before arriving at the current location. Sir Osbert concludes that Boll was targeting his confidential files regarding a valuable formula and asserts that the man seen climbing the ivy was indeed Boll. As Inspector Batter leaves to make a phone call, Jimmy Cecil tells Eileen Bündel that he finds Bartholomew's actions suspicious, specifically questioning why Bartholomew sent Cut to throw the gun.
